docs.title: Lesson overview
docs.text:
Description Add a short description of the training materail should be inserted here.

Prerequisites To be able to follow this course, learners should have knowledge in:  1. First requirement  2. Second requirement

Learning Outcomes: By the end of the course, learners will be able to:  1. First item  2. Second item

Target Audience: Researchers, undergraduate students, postgraduate students, etc…

Level: Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced

License: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License

Funding: This project has received funding from [name of funders].

docs.text:
Please cite as:

  1. Enter your citation here.
  2. Geert van Geest, Elin Kronander, Jose Alejandro Romero Herrera, Nadja Žlender, & Alexia Cardona. (2023). The ELIXIR Training Lesson Template - Developing Training Together (v1.0.0-alpha). Zenodo. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7913092.

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text:
Here’s a lit of used keywords:

  1. C. Lang. Learning analytics. In C. Lang, G. Siemens, A. Wise, and D. Gašević, editors, Handbook of Learning Analytics. Society for Learning Analytics Research (SoLAR), 2 edition, 2022. URL: https://solaresearch.org/wp-content/uploads/hla22/HLA22_Chapter_1_Lang.pdf. ↩

  2. M. Soncin, T. Agasisti, F. Frattini, A. Patrucco, and M. Pero. The costs, quality, and scalability of blended learning in postgraduate management education. Journal of Management Education, 46(6):1052–1085, 2022. URL: https://doi.org/10.1177/10525629221103826, doi:10.1177/10525629221103826. ↩

  3. M. E. Ray, M. J. Rudolph, and K. K. Daugherty. Bloom’s taxonomy in health professions education: associations with exam scores, clinical reasoning, and instructional effectiveness. Current Pharmacy Teaching and Learning, 17(11):102444, 2025. URL: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cptl.2025.102444, doi:10.1016/j.cptl.2025.102444. ↩

  4. R. Grossman, E. Salas, D. Pavlas, and M. A. Rosen. Using instructional features to enhance demonstration-based training in management education. Academy of Management Learning \\& Education, 12(2):219–243, 2013. URL: https://doi.org/10.5465/amle.2011.0527, doi:10.5465/amle.2011.0527. ↩

  5. M. Seren Smith, S. Warnes, and A. Vanhoestenberghe. Scenario-based learning. 2018. URL: https://discovery.ucl.ac.uk/id/eprint/10050606/. ↩

  6. S. K. Ghani. Role of Learning Management Systems for Formative Assessment in Higher Education. PhD thesis, Université d’Ottawa/University of Ottawa, 2019. PhD Thesis. URL: https://ruor.uottawa.ca/items/9bbf3820-35f8-41b7-86d3-61bf7c9263e7. ↩

  7. A. Scutelnicu and others. Template-based course design and reuse in digital education. 2019. URL: https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/abs/10.1177/0144739419852759, doi:10.1177/0144739419852759. ↩

  8. T. Gaddis. Templates to improve instructional consistency and reduce preparation time. 2022. URL: https://files.eric.ed.gov/fulltext/EJ1367187.pdf.mkdocs ↩

docs.title: 1.1 First subtopic
docs.text:
Here you can enter text and create inline citations by using the bibtex plugin. Add your references in references.bib, and cite by adding the @refid inside brackets like this ``

You can also embed videos from a local source (with a relative path) or from an url (like youtube). To use a youtube URL, just attach the ID of the video to a youtube embedded video link: https://youtube.com/embed/. For example, the Elixir training video https://youtu.be/oAD8FdGf8tI has the ID oAD8FdGf8tI, so the final link would be:

![type:video](https://youtube.com/embed/oAD8FdGf8tI)
